spec.reasoning: The task references ITEM, DATE, and QTY columns by name, plus sheet names 'Data' and 'Transpose'. We rename columns and sheets to break hardcoded references: ITEM→PART_NUM, QTY→QUANTITY (the instruction mentions QTY so the agent must find the renamed column), and sheets Data→Source, Transpose→Unpivot. We also rename supporting columns (DESCRIPTION→SPEC, UOM→UNIT, SUPPLIER_NAME→VENDOR_NAME, etc.) for consistency. A distractor column LEAD_TIME is added after the last column on the Source sheet to test whether the agent incorrectly includes extra columns. Note: the instruction text says 'ITEM, DATE, and QTY' — ITEM and QTY are column names being renamed, while DATE refers to the date columns (which are timestamps, not a single header called DATE), so the rename is safe. We keep instruction_paraphrase false to avoid meaning drift; the instruction still references old names but the agent must map them to the new schema.
